behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type is pure scoring logic: inputs are transaction fields + category config, output is numeric score. The failure is local threshold/weight logic, not module handoff. Rejection gate: I considered Integration, but behavior text only requires correct scoring for threshold exceedance and does not require DB/network/subprocess/model-quality dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Test the scoring function/object directly with category-specific thresholds at boundary values (threshold-1, threshold, threshold+1) and assert exact numeric risk score changes with no I/O.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type is real model judgment quality: the behavior is about detecting a fraud pattern that rule thresholds miss. A stubbed/mocked model would hide this failure because it can be made to always return expected labels. Rejection gate: I considered Workflow, but behavior text describes one model decision step, not multiple agentic steps chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on a labeled fraud slice containing cross-geo rapid small-purchase patterns with rule thresholds untriggered, and assert fraud probability/flag quality (e.g., recall on this slice above agreed threshold).

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type is local module wiring: scoring logic must interact with transaction-history retrieval and time-window filtering. A pure unit test over a prebuilt list can miss real handoff/query/window-boundary bugs. Integration-brake check: no real OS/network/subprocess is required, no AI/ML judgment is required, and no multi-agentic sequence is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Use a local test DB (or in-memory repo implementation) with timestamped transactions across users and window edges; invoke the scoring service velocity path and assert per-user counts include/exclude correct records at boundaries.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type is cross-module decision wiring: risk-model output must propagate into hold logic even when scoring output is false. A unit test of a standalone boolean OR is too narrow and can miss mapping/orchestration regressions between modules. Integration-brake check: real OS/network/subprocess is not required, correctness does not depend on model quality (fixed outputs suffice), and this is not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Wire model adapters + decision service in-process, feed deterministic outputs (risk=true, scoring=false), and assert transaction status transitions to held/manual review; verify decision metadata reflects OR behavior.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency type is output-contract wiring across components: scorer result, risk result, and review payload assembly. Real failures are typically dropped/mis-mapped fields at module boundaries, which unit-testing one formatter may miss. Integration-brake check: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency is required, no AI quality dependency is required (fixed probability input is enough), and no multi-agentic sequence is required.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the in-process pipeline that builds reviewer-facing output from both model results and assert payload contains numeric score, fraud probability, and per-model flag/reason fields with correct values.
